riddles.riddle: I was the world's first computer programmer, having written an algorithm for the Analytical Engine in the mid-1800s. Who am I?
riddles.description: Correct! Ada Lovelace saw the potential for computers to do more than just crunch numbers, envisioning them as tools for creativity and logic.

riddles.riddle: In 1947, a physical moth was found trapped in a relay of the Harvard Mark II computer. This incident popularized what 3-letter term for a technical error?
riddles.description: Exactly! Grace Hopper's team literally 'debugged' the machine by removing the moth and taping it into their logbook.

riddles.riddle: I am the foundational protocol of the World Wide Web, used to transmit data between a client and a server. I usually come before a '://' in your browser.
riddles.description: You've got it. Hypertext Transfer Protocol is the language of the web, allowing us to fetch the very resources you're looking at right now.

riddles.riddle: Created by Linus Torvalds in 2005, I am the most widely used version control system in the world. What is my name?
riddles.description: Spot on. Whether you are branching, merging, or committing, Git is the backbone of modern collaborative software development.

riddles.riddle: I am a simple, logic-based test proposed in 1950 to determine if a machine can exhibit intelligent behavior indistinguishable from a human. What am I called?
riddles.description: Excellent. Alan Turing's 'Imitation Game' remains a philosophical cornerstone in the field of Artificial Intelligence.
